explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'PSVMode', and the 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' is reached, and no inspiration pause is requested (represented by '!inspiratoryPauseButton'), and the 'RMButton' is set (indicating the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI), then in the next state (X), the 'RM' (Recruitment Maneuver) must start and be active. This directly translates the conditional statement 'At the end of an inspiration phase if no inspiration pause is required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, a Recruitment Maneuver shall start' within the PSV Mode context.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is operating in PSV Mode, and the inspiratory phase has ended, and an inspiratory pause is not active or required, and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) has been set via the GUI, then the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must become active in the very next state. This directly translates the condition 'At the end of an inspiration phase if no inspiration pause is required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI' into the antecedent, and 'a Recruitment Maneuver shall start' into the consequent 'X RM', meaning RM becomes true in the next step.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode), and it is the end of an inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and no inspiratory pause is required (!inspiratoryPause), and the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI (RMButton), then in the next state (X), the Recruitment Maneuver shall start (RM). This captures the immediate consequence implied by 'shall start' upon the conjunction of all specified conditions.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'PSVMode' AND the inspiratory phase has ended ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd') AND an inspiratory pause is NOT required ('!inspiratoryPause') AND the Recruitment Maneuver is set via the GUI ('RMButton'), THEN it must eventually be the case that a Recruitment Maneuver ('RM') becomes active. This captures the conditional requirement that a Recruitment Maneuver shall start under the specified circumstances within the PSV Mode.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in PSV Mode, and at the end of an inspiration phase, there is no inspiratory pause required, and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, then the Recruitment Maneuver shall start in the very next state. This directly translates the temporal and conditional dependencies described in the natural language statement.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode ('PSVMode') AND it's the end of the inspiratory phase ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd') AND an inspiratory pause is NOT required ('!inspiratoryPause') AND the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI ('RMButton'), THEN in the next state (X), a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall become active ('RM'). This precisely captures the conditionality and the immediate consequence described in the natural language.

The property states that globally, when the system is in 'PSVMode' (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode), and simultaneously the 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' (end of an inspiration phase) is reached, an 'inspiratoryPause' is not active or required (!inspiratoryPause), and 'RMButton' (Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI) is active, then 'RM' (Recruitment Maneuver) shall start in the very next state (X RM). This directly translates the given conditions and the required immediate action.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode), and it is the end of an inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and there is no inspiratory pause active or required (!inspiratoryPause), and the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI (RMButton), then in the very next state (X), the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall be active. This precisely captures the conditionality and the immediate 'shall start' aspect of the requirement, indicating a transition to the RM active state.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode) and the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and no inspiratory pause is required (!inspiratoryPause), and the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI (RMButton), then in the next state (X), a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall start. This captures the conditional activation of the Recruitment Maneuver based on the specified conditions at the end of an inspiratory phase in PSV mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode), and it's the end of an inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and an inspiration pause is not active (!inspiratoryPause), and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI (RMButton), then in the next state (X), the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must become active. This directly translates the conditional requirement for RM to start under the specified circumstances.
